pi_items.verified_value: [5.0, 15.0] is plausible for back-row wake losses
pi_items.source: Web search on wind farm wake effects: typical wake losses range 5-15% for downwind turbines. Verifier expects Wake_Loss in percentage form calculated as (Front_CF - Back_CF) / Front_CF * 100. At 5% loss, Back CF would be ~95% of Front CF; at 15% loss, Back CF would be ~85% of Front CF.

privileged_info_summary: The wind_farm_capacity_analysis task is for a 50 MW wind farm (20 × GE 2.5 MW turbines) located in central Iowa, analyzing 2023 annual production data. Data is synthetic (not from a real farm) but uses realistic parameters: annual hours = 8760, PPA price = $45/MWh, performance thresholds of 28% (underperforming), 32% (review), 5-15% expected wake loss from back-row turbines. Iowa is a leading wind energy state with typical capacity factors of 33-40%, making the 28-32% threshold range appropriate for identifying underperforming units. The task requires the agent to complete analysis sheets with SUMIF formulas, capacity factor calculations, revenue projections, and wake loss analysis across 20 turbines and 12 months.
